_selection.purpose: Curated list of popular, npx-runnable MCP servers used to collect ecosystem baseline data for jig's percentile grading and the 'State of MCP Servers' report.
criteria: Runnable via `npx -y <package> [args]` on a stock Node 22 machine with NO API key, auth token, or config file required to complete the handshake + tools/list. A server that LISTS tools without credentials but needs a key to CALL them qualifies — the census never calls tools.

Actively published: latest version released within ~12 months of the collection date (a handful of famous-but-deprecated official servers are kept deliberately as ecosystem-drift data points and are noted as such).
Popular: every entry had measurable npm traffic at selection time (last-month downloads ranged from ~700 to ~24M; the median entry was well above 20k/month).

Diverse authorship: Anthropic/official @modelcontextprotocol servers are capped at 8 of 50 so the dataset is not one org. The remaining 42 span independent authors, framework vendors, and infrastructure companies.
Servers that need a directory/path argument (e.g. server-filesystem) carry the placeholder token "{TMPDIR}" in `args`; the census script substitutes a freshly-created temp directory at run time.

sources.npm: Discovered via the public npm registry search API (keywords: 'mcp server', 'modelcontextprotocol'), ranked by last-month download count.
sources.registry: Canonical top entries on public MCP registry/directory listings (mcp.so, Smithery, Glama).

_selection.notes: Inclusion here is NOT an endorsement and NOT a claim the server works — the census records honest pass/fail. Roughly a third are expected to fail the handshake (missing startup credentials, platform-specific, or requiring a live backend); those failures are reported, never silently dropped.

servers.note: Official reference server exercising every MCP feature (tools/resources/prompts).
Official filesystem server; requires an allowed-directory argument (temp dir substituted).
Official knowledge-graph memory server; no config.
Official structured-reasoning server; no config.
Official GitHub server (deprecated; superseded by the Go github-mcp-server). Kept as a drift data point; may require a token at startup.
Official Slack server (deprecated). Kept as a drift data point; likely needs a bot token at startup.
Official GitLab server (deprecated). Kept as a drift data point.
Official EverArt image server (deprecated). Kept as a drift data point.
Microsoft Playwright browser-automation server; the single most-downloaded MCP server on npm.
Upstash Context7 docs-retrieval server; widely deployed, no key for basic use.
Popular terminal/desktop-automation server.
Official ESLint MCP server; runs locally against a project.
Mantine UI documentation server.
AntV chart-generation server.
assistant-ui documentation server.
Code Runner server (formulahendry, author of the VS Code extension).
Local git-operations server.
Shopify developer documentation/tooling server.
Mapbox geospatial server; may require a token at startup.
draw.io diagram server.
Xcode build server; macOS-only, expected to fail on Linux CI (platform data point).
iOS Simulator control server; macOS-only, expected to fail on Linux (platform data point).
Browser devtools bridge server.
URL fetch/scrape server.
SearXNG meta-search server; requires SEARXNG_URL env, expected to fail at startup (config data point).
MediaWiki server.
Nx monorepo server.
Shortcut (project mgmt) server; likely needs a token at startup.
Official Notion server; likely needs an integration token at startup.
Official Sentry server; likely needs an auth token at startup.
Tavily search server; requires TAVILY_API_KEY.
Brave Search server; requires an API key.
Firecrawl web-scraping server; requires an API key.
Official Stripe server; likely needs a secret key at startup.
Perplexity search server; requires an API key.
Official HubSpot server; likely needs a token at startup.
DataForSEO server; requires credentials.
Apify Actors server; likely needs an API token at startup.
Official Heroku server; likely needs auth at startup.
Official LaunchDarkly server; likely needs an API key at startup.
ClickUp server; requires an API token.
MySQL server; requires a reachable database, expected to fail (backend data point).
Kubernetes server (containers/Red Hat lineage); may list tools without a live cluster.
Official Azure server; large tool surface, may need auth at startup.
GrowthBook feature-flag server; likely needs an API key.
New Relic observability server; likely needs credentials.
Kubernetes server (Flux159 lineage); a second, independent k8s implementation for comparison.
Microsoft Clarity analytics server; likely needs a token.
Serper search/scrape server; requires an API key.
Dynatrace observability server; likely needs credentials.
